title: fix: use dedicated POLLI_VECTOR_ID/POLLI_VECTOR_KEY secrets
author: Itachi-1824
pr_body_excerpt: The MYCELI cloudflare secrets are scoped for something else — first incremental run after merge hit a 401 from the Vectorize API. Switched to dedicated secrets for this workflow instead of debugging what the old ones are actually meant for.

gist.headline: Vectors found their keys
gist.blurb: The code-embedding garden now uses its own set of keys. Fewer confused secret drawers, more vectors finding their proper home.
gist.summary: Updated the Vectorize code-embeddings GitHub workflow to use dedicated POLLI_VECTOR_ID and POLLI_VECTOR_KEY secrets instead of MYCELI-scoped credentials.
gist.impact: Incremental code-embedding runs can authenticate with the Vectorize API using credentials scoped specifically for this workflow.
gist.visual_concept: A cozy garden-shed workflow scene: Polly acts as a careful keykeeper, matching two labeled vector keys to a glowing retro terminal that turns code sheets into tidy floating vector seeds. Small code-plants and a successful terminal check represent reliable embedding runs.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene in a garden-shed hackerspace where Polly, the cute yellow-and-dark-brown striped bee mascot with pink blush, light blue wings, and a tiny locksmith hat, carefully inserts two small keys labeled "VECTOR ID" and "VECTOR KEY" into a lime green (#ecf874) glowing retro terminal while code sheets become floating vector seeds among flower pots. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, with subtle CRT glow and a small code-plant garden; nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
